name: partial_sorting
title: Partial cell sorting
question: Does violating the Young condition (large gamma_ld=7.5) stall sorting at a partially-sorted, trapped state?
hypothesis: Light cells cannot fully surround dark; clusters trap heterotypic inclusions and never form a monolayer, sorting logarithmically slow.
objective: Swap J_ll/J_ld so gamma_ld=7.5, simulate from a random IC to 2000 MCS, and compare against normal sorting to demonstrate incomplete sorting.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 22+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=11, J_dd=2, J_ld=14, J_lM=16, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+7.5, gamma_lM=+10.5, gamma_dM=+15); T=5.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 22, Fig 23, Fig 24.
claim: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).
expected_behavior: No complete light monolayer forms by 2000 MCS
Light-dark interface plateaus above the normal-sorting curve (Fig 24)

embed_visualizations.name: All paper figures
embed_visualizations.title: Partial cell sorting — full figure set

findings.statement: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Partial cell sorting example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 22, Fig 23, Fig 24.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

behavior_tests.name: Partial sorting stalls: coarsening but no monolayer
behavior_tests.description: Paper: Fig 22-24 — Young condition fails; clusters trap inclusions, no monolayer. Pass criterion: light-dark contact drops (coarsening) BUT light never owns the surface — no monolayer (corr_lM<0.75, vs >0.85 for complete sorting). Measured from the reproduced run: frac_ld 0.400→0.126, corr_lM_final=0.618 (no monolayer).
behavior_tests.pass_if: light-dark contact drops (coarsening) BUT light never owns the surface — no monolayer (corr_lM<0.75, vs >0.85 for complete sorting)
behavior_tests.paper_reference: Fig 22-24 — Young condition fails; clusters trap inclusions, no monolayer

derived.insight: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).

key_metrics.label: Partial sorting stalls: coarsening but no monolayer
